data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was an emergent property of synchronized audit cycles across palace economies using similar scribal accounting technologies, creating temporal correlation of fiscal stress without shared external shock", "mechanism": "Isomorphic administrative systems → correlated internal contradictions (debt accumulation, patronage inflation, tax-base erosion) → simultaneous but causally independent state failures → opportunistic exploitation by peripheral actors (Sea Peoples) misread as unified cause", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "temporal clustering illusion"], "excludes": ["Egypt (different fiscal-military integration)", "genuine external shocks", "climate determinism"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Palace economies showed asynchronous collapse with 50+ year stagger rather than near-simultaneity", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Linear B and cuneiform administrative systems substantively differed in fiscal forecasting and debt management", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Sea Peoples invasions demonstrably preceded and triggered palace collapses rather than postdating them", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Radically deflationary account: denies unified cause for 'collapse' itself while accepting synchronous timing as artifact of institutional isomorphism; treats Egypt as different regime type (temple-military rather than palace-redistributive); inspired by skeptic stance demanding we question whether simultaneous collapse requires single cause"}
